channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As ranks of uninsured grow, charity care can be hard to come by at many hospitals
item.description: An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.
item.content:encoded:
An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.

(Image credit: Anthony Souffle)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: Marketers say NAD+ pills and infusions can boost longevity. What's the evidence?
item.description: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.content:encoded: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers arrive in the U.S. after one tests positive for hantavirus
item.description: One of the 17 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.
item.content:encoded:
One of the 17 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Doomscrolling too much? Try these tips to put the phone down and plug into real life
item.description: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.content:encoded: NPR Life Kit's Marielle Segarra provides tips to help you reduce your screentime.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: A chocolate laboratory in Italy will be good for chocolate eaters — and farmers
item.description: The chocolate biz is raising the bar with a lab to assess cacao beans from around the world. (Talk about a sweet gig!) Consumers and farmers stand to benefit from the "Standard of Excellence" program.
item.content:encoded:
The chocolate biz is raising the bar with a lab to assess cacao beans from around the world. (Talk about a sweet gig!) Consumers and farmers stand to benefit from the "Standard of Excellence" program.

(Image credit: Valerio Muscella for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Happy Mother's Day to the kindest mom. P.S. Your kindness annoyed me when I was a kid
item.description: She and her siblings had to make tea for and share food with every visitor. That did not make her happy. Today she has a different perspective. So she finally asked her mom: What made you so kind?
item.content:encoded:
She and her siblings had to make tea for and share food with every visitor. That did not make her happy. Today she has a different perspective. So she finally asked her mom: What made you so kind?

(Image credit: Family photo)
item.dc:creator: Esther Ndumi Ngumbi

item.title: How a pill approved 25 years ago transformed cancer treatment
item.description: When the Food and Drug Administration approved Gleevec to treat a form of leukemia in 2001, it ushered in a new era in cancer care.
item.content:encoded:
When the Food and Drug Administration approved Gleevec to treat a form of leukemia in 2001, it ushered in a new era in cancer care.

(Image credit: Kristyna Wentz-Graff)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Why saying hello to strangers can be good for you
item.description: A study establishes that "social ties" — a fancy way of saying being nice to other, even those you don't know — has benefits. A teacher asked her students to test the thesis in real life.
item.content:encoded: A study establishes that "social ties" — a fancy way of saying being nice to other, even those you don't know — has benefits. A teacher asked her students to test the thesis in real life.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Lack of U.S. response to hantavirus outbreak worries public health experts
item.description: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.content:encoded: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Contact tracing could be key in halting the spread of hantavirus. Here's how it works
item.description: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.
item.content:encoded:
More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.

(Image credit: lakshmiprasad S/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
